context: A standard, friendly question used to ask someone about their profession or line of work.
A common phrase used to describe a standard corporate or administrative work environment.
Terms used for a supervisor. 'Chefe' is masculine, and 'Chefa' is common in casual feminine contexts.
Refers to the people you share an office space or professional team duties with.
A routine schedule reminder regarding a corporate presentation or team sync.
A primary professional communication action executed daily on a computer.
Refers to the hardware equipment assigned to you by your employer for work purposes.

back: I need to finish this report.
context: An expression used when focusing heavily on paperwork or documenting data before a deadline.

back: To work from home / remote work
context: The modern practice of carrying out professional duties from your personal residence.
The designated personal rest hour during the official shift to eat and step away from tasks.

back: I am very busy right now.
context: Used by a male speaker to politely defer non-urgent interactions. Female speakers say 'ocupada'.
The plastic identification card worn to pass through security gates at corporate offices.
The final formal step of officially finalizing a job offer, partnership, or deal.
The baseline monthly monetary compensation received by an employee for their labor.
The highly anticipated calendar date when monthly wages hit your bank account.
Staying late at the workplace beyond your standard scheduled hours to log additional shifts.
The screening meeting evaluation phase with human resources to secure a career vacancy.
The summary document highlighting your professional history and credentials during hiring.
An electronic verification method used to sign official corporate paperwork instantly.
The specific task of logging your exact entry and exit work times on a company timecard device.
Refers to digital table tools like Excel files used extensively to balance metrics or figures.
The designated corporate chamber where executive teams deliberate on projects together.
Refers to a freshly launched corporate initiative or fresh campaign assignment assignment.
Refers to a buyer or client who is highly pleased with your business outcomes or services.

back: To call IT support / technical help
context: Action step taken when office software, internet routing, or computers break down.
Advancing upward into a higher tier position with increased career responsibility and income.
A small printed card displaying your company title, contact email, and mobile info.
The welcoming entryway zone where corporate guests report upon entering a building structure.
The exact final calendar date or hour target on which a task must be fully handed in.
Sorting through documents, storage setups, or folders to keep things clean and functional.
Encouraging, helpful evaluations received from peers or management on excellent work.
Collaborative efforts between multiple professionals working to execute a goal smoothly.
Concluding your professional working life for good after long years of lifelong career dedication.
A classic, encouraging sign-off phrase used with team colleagues as Friday shifts conclude.
A supportive phrase used by a lead or peer to praise and motivate the entire department group.
